patient_data.chief_complaint: Severe epigastric pain radiating to the back for 6 hours, worse after eating
symptoms: Severe constant epigastric pain radiating to the back
Pain worse when lying flat, slightly better leaning forward
Nausea with multiple episodes of vomiting
patient_data.history: Known gallstones (asymptomatic, declined cholecystectomy), alcohol use 2-3 beers/week, hypertriglyceridemia, obesity BMI 34

lab_results.RUQ_ultrasound: Multiple gallstones, dilated common bile duct 10mm

should_detect: NPO — aggressive IV fluid resuscitation (Ringer's lactate 250-500mL/hr initial)
Pain control — avoid morphine (allergy noted), use hydromorphone or ketorolac
Ranson criteria / BISAP score for severity stratification
MRCP or EUS to evaluate CBD stone
Urgent ERCP if cholangitis develops (elevated bilirubin + fever)
Plan cholecystectomy during same admission after resolution
red_flags: Elevated bilirubin with dilated CBD (biliary obstruction)
